title: Configure environment variable in .kube file that can be used by application manifest/yml
body:
### Feature request description

**Use Case**
I have an application that requires mounting the Podman socket as a volume into a container. Is this something that can be configured while using Quadlet without having to manually or using a script for updating user value as we can't know the location of podman's socket statically.   Here is the code snippet as follows

```
        - name: pman
          image: ghcr.io/fnndsc/pman:5.0.0.a5
          ports:
            - name: pman
              containerPort: 5010
          env:
            - name: CONTAINER_ENV
              value: docker
            - name: STORAGE_TYPE
              value: docker_local_volume
            - name: VOLUME_NAME
              value: minichris-storebase
            - name: STOREBASE
            - name: SECRET_KEY
              value: secret
          volumeMounts:
            - mountPath: /var/run/docker.sock
              name: podman-socket

      # Let SELinux allow us to access the Podman socket
      # https://unix.stackexchange.com/a/595152
      securityContext:
        seLinuxOptions:
          type: spc_t

      volumes:
        - name: storebase
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: minichris-storebase
        - name: podman-socket
          hostPath:
            # pman needs to run containers, and it does so by communicating with the Podman socket.
            # This means that the Podman system service must be running, and the socket's path
            # must be known.
            # The hard-coded path below is replaced with the user-specific value by minichris.sh
            path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
            type: Socket
```

### Suggest potential solution

The value for `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock` be set as an environment variable in unit file so that it can be accepted by the pod.yml 

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the issue, @veniceofcode!

I think it makes quite a lot of sense to support expanding environment variables in K8s YAML. As mentioned in the description, Quadlet is a good use case where some expanding paths and volumes etc. would render the YAML more portable.
We need to make sure to differentiate between host environment variables and container ones (for which the support was added here: #19630)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ygalblum do we need a jira as well for this issue?

> @ygalblum do we need a jira as well for this issue?

AFAIK podman issues are tracked only here
@ygalblum what needs to be changed here?

@rhatdan IIUC the change you did in #19630, use the`Env` section of the `SpecGenerator` to create a mapping function that is later used to expand variables used in `Entrypoint` and `Command`.

Now, this request adds environment variables to the mapping options and also extends the list of fields that should be expanded (in the example it is the `path` of the `hostPath` typed volume).

All this can be achieved by using the same methods already available. But, first, we should decide which fields we want to allow expanding (as this is handle at the line level) and with which type of environment variables (container, host or both). Once this is decided we can make the actual change.

Also, note that the `tryReadVariableName` used by the `Expand` looks for the pattern `$(VAR_NAME)`. So, if we are to use it, referencing the host environment variables will need to take the same form. Using different patters (e.g. `$VAR_NAME`) will require implementing the functions separately since these functions are vendoered
